The total response time function sums the individual times for each database line.
MySQL contributes a term based on its quadratic and exponential growth line.
Oracle adds a logarithmic component with a reciprocal fraction term line.
Sybase provides a power function with a square root subtraction line.
The total function combines these three distinct mathematical expressions line.
Optimization requires finding the partial derivatives with respect to alpha line.
We also need the partial derivative concerning the beta variable line.
Setting both derivatives to zero yields a system of equations line.
Solving this system numerically gives the specific optimal proportions line.
The calculated alpha value minimizes the overall system latency line.
The calculated beta value further reduces the total response time line.
Substituting the given constants into the equation produces a numeric line.
The result shows that load balancing significantly improves performance line.
The original data suggests a non uniform distribution is best line.
